data.title: ticket list --format=llm omits tags field
data.description:
## Observed
`.claude/scripts/dso ticket list --format=llm` emits JSONL with shortened keys but the `tg` (tags) field is empty/missing for tickets that actually have tags. Verified against open epics: llm format shows `tg=[]` for every ticket, while `ticket show <id>` returns the real tags array (e.g., `["brainstorm:complete"]`, `["scrutiny:pending"]`, `["CLI_user"]`).

## Impact
Consumers that need to filter by tags cannot use the efficient llm-format bulk query; they must fall back to N+1 `ticket show` subprocess calls. Example today: filtering 90 open epics by `not brainstorm:complete` required 90 separate subprocess invocations instead of one.

## Expected
The `--format=llm` output should include the tags array under a stable short key (e.g., `tg`), populated from the compiled state so tag-based filtering works without a second pass.

## Repro
1. Tag an open ticket: `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag <id> foo`
2. `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list --format=llm | grep <id>`
3. Observe `tg` is empty or absent; `ticket show <id>` shows `"tags": ["foo"]`

## Notes
- Reported interactively during a session that needed to filter open epics by tag.
- Related to the CLI overhead concern tracked in epic 78fc-3858 (ticket CLI subprocess overhead).
